# School Cafeteria Menu App

This project is a School Cafeteria Menu App developed with claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-thinking-32k:

* main.py: generated by claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-thinking-32k tested and modified by Emily
* emilyguo.py: helper functions coded by Emily Guo

  * calculate median of input values
  * calculate the bayesian average value
  * calculate histogram
* Three test scripts for emilyguo.py

### Instruction to the Team

* **Please avoid deleting or changing code in main.py. Talk to the team first.**
* **Please keep your function or subroutine code in your own Python file.**
* **Additions to main.py should be minimal and well-commented.**
* **Always separate your handwritten code from AI-generated code with comments.**

### News

* **\[2026-04-11]** Second release by Emily, complete function update
* **\[2026-04-06]** First release by Emily

### Main Features:

* Menu Browsing (Home Page)
Displays food items with photos, prices, descriptions, and star ratings
Shows shopping cart count
Allows adding items to cart directly from the menu
Displays the chef recomnendation based on bayesian rating

* Item Details Page
Shows detailed information about selected menu items (including average rating and median rating)
Allows users to submit ratings (1-5 stars)
Displays all previous customer ratings with histogram

* Shopping Cart \& Checkout Tracks items and quantities in cart
Shows itemized order with pricing
Allows removing items
Places orders with confirmation and clears cart

* Owner Management (Password Protected) Password-protected page
Allows cafeteria owners to add new dishes to the menu
Validates input and prevents duplicate dish IDs
Uses session state to persist data (cart, menu items, ratings, current page)
Includes navigation between different pages/views
Pre-populated with 3 sample menu items (burger, pizza, salad)

### Required

* Python 3.12.3
* Streamlit, version 1.56.0

### How to run

In the terminal, go to the folder containing main.py, then run:

\~\~\~
streamlit run main.py
\~\~\~

If that doesn’t work, try:

\~\~\~
python -m streamlit run main.py
\~\~\~
